behaviors.description: The conversation history passed to the LLM is trimmed to the most recent 10 messages when the full history exceeds the context window.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: this is mainly a module handoff behavior (history selection -> prompt assembly -> LLM request payload), which unit tests can miss at wiring boundaries. REAL-INFRA: no real OS/network/subprocess is required; a stubbed LLM transport is sufficient. LLM-DEP: correctness does not depend on model judgment, only on what payload is sent. REJECTION-GATE: System was considered but rejected because behavior text is about trimming before send ("passed to the LLM is trimmed"), not real external contract validation.
behaviors.test_description: Run the chat service with in-process wiring and a stub LLM client that records request payloads; feed >10 prior messages and assert only the latest 10 (correct order) are included in the actual payload sent by the orchestrator.

behaviors.description: The generated response addresses the customer's question rather than producing a generic or off-topic reply.
behaviors.justification: LLM-DEP: this is directly about output quality/relevance of model generations; deterministic stubs would hide failure. MOCK-HIDE: mocked responses can always be made on-topic, giving false confidence. MULTI-STEP: not necessarily a multi-agentic pipeline requirement in this behavior; a single generation quality check is enough. REJECTION-GATE: Workflow was considered but rejected because behavior text only requires one generation to be on-topic, not sequential model decisions.
behaviors.test_description: Execute real model calls on a representative prompt set and evaluate whether responses answer the user question (human rubric or calibrated evaluator), tracking pass rate across repeated runs to account for nondeterminism.

behaviors.description: The tone re-check correctly identifies when a response is too formal for a casual-tier customer and triggers regeneration.
behaviors.justification: LLM-DEP: correctness of "correctly identifies ... too formal" depends on model judgment (or equivalent AI scoring), which stubs cannot validate. Integration-only tests can verify trigger plumbing but not real tone-detection correctness. MULTI-STEP: regeneration is invoked, but the core claim is single decision correctness (flag too-formal) plus trigger, so Agentic is the lowest additional level. REJECTION-GATE: Workflow was considered but rejected because behavior text does not require validating quality across multiple model decisions end-to-end, only identification + trigger.
behaviors.test_description: Provide casual-tier cases with intentionally formal drafts and run the real tone-check path; assert the system flags them and issues a regeneration call, with repeated trials to account for model variance.

behaviors.description: The full exchange (user message, generated response, any regenerations) is persisted to the database with correct timestamps.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: this is cross-module state persistence (orchestrator -> persistence layer -> readback). Unit tests on serializers/repo methods are insufficient alone. REAL-INFRA: can be validated with local/in-process DB wiring and round-trip query; no mandatory real network/OS primitive is required by the behavior text. REJECTION-GATE: System was considered due side-effect verification, but rejected because behavior specifically targets DB persistence and can be proven by integration round-trip readback without external service contract checks.
behaviors.test_description: Run an end-to-end in-process service flow against a test database, trigger both normal and regeneration paths, then read back stored records and assert all exchange entries and timestamp fields are present and correctly ordered.

behaviors.description: When the tone check triggers regeneration, the second response replaces the first — the customer never sees the rejected draft.
behaviors.justification: ROUND-TRIP EFFECT RULE / SYSTEM TRIAD #3: this is an externally visible side effect claim (what the customer sees), so calling internals is insufficient; must verify via real request/response boundary readback. MOCK-HIDE: in-process mocks can hide response-streaming or boundary ordering bugs. LLM-DEP: model quality itself is not the core assertion here if trigger can be forced; the key risk is external visibility/contract behavior. REJECTION-GATE: Agentic was considered but rejected because behavior text is about replacement visibility ("customer never sees"), not whether model content is good.
behaviors.test_description: Invoke the real chat API/UI boundary on a scenario where regeneration is triggered, then assert the externally returned/streamed response contains only the regenerated text and excludes the rejected draft, while internal logs may still retain both.
